eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
eve: noun. The day or night before, usually used for holidays, such as Christmas Eve.
jeu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Jonkor Bourmataguil.
jive: verb. To deceive; to be deceptive.
jun: noun. A monetary unit of North Korea and formerly of South Korea, equal to one hundredth of a won.
lee: noun. A protected cove or harbor, out of the wind.
lei: noun. A garland of flowers in Hawaii.
leu: noun. The unit of currency of Romania, equal to one hundred bani.
lev: noun. The currency of Bulgaria from 1880 to 2025; divided into 100 stotinki.
levin: noun. Lightning; a bolt of lightning; also, a bright flame or light.
lie: verb. To rest in a horizontal position on a surface.
lin: verb. To desist, to stop, to cease.
line: noun. A path through two or more points (compare ‘segment’); a continuous mark, including as made by a pen; any path, curved or straight.
live: verb. To be alive; to have life.
liven: verb. To cause to be more lively, or to become more lively.
lune: noun. A fit of lunacy or madness; a period of frenzy; a crazy or unreasonable freak.
nee: interjection. no, used to express no as a quantity, i.e. not any, like German kein /Dutch geen /French rien.
unlive: verb. To bereave or deprive of life; make unalive.
unveil: verb. To remove a veil from; to uncover; to reveal something hidden.
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veil: noun. Something hung up or spread out to hide or protect the face, or hide an object from view; usually of gauze, crepe, or similar
vein: noun. A blood vessel that transports blood from the capillaries back to the heart.
venue: noun. A theater, auditorium, arena, or other area designated for sporting or entertainment events.
venule: noun. A small vein, especially one that connects capillaries to a larger vein.
vie: verb. To fight for superiority; to contend; to compete eagerly so as to gain something.
vine: noun. Any plant whose stem requires support and which climbs by tendrils or twining.
eli: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nding.
elne: verb. To strengthen; hearten; comfort; encourage.
elven: noun. Originally, a female elf, a fairy, a nymph; any elf.
jeel: noun. Alternative form of jheel (“wetland area in India”).
juvie: noun. Synonym of juvenile hall (youth detention centre).
lue: verb. To sift using a sieve, particularly in mining tin or silver.
nul: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nusa Laut.
uji: noun. A Japanese clan or kin group of the Kofun period.
ule: noun. A Mexican and Central American tree (Castilla elastica), related to the breadfruit tree, whose milky juice contains caoutchouc.
vlei: noun. A shallow wetland or minor lake, generally a seasonal one, or the lowland where such a wetland appears seasonally.
vuln: verb. Of a pelican, to wound (oneself) by biting at the breast.
